headline: Sudden Vision Loss -- Seek Care Immediately
subheadline: Sudden vision loss can be a medical emergency. Dr. Rajeswari is available to evaluate urgent cases promptly in KK Nagar.
metaDescription: Sudden vision loss requires urgent evaluation. Contact Dr. Rajeswari in KK Nagar, Chennai immediately. Call now.

whyPatientsChoose.title: Urgent Assessment for Sight-Threatening Conditions
whyPatientsChoose.description: Sudden vision changes are treated as the emergency they may be -- prompt evaluation, no delay.

whyPatientsChoose.title: Direct Access, No Triage Delay
whyPatientsChoose.description: Call directly and reach the clinic immediately -- no waiting on hold or being passed between departments.

symptoms: Sudden loss of vision in one or both eyes
A curtain-like shadow moving across your vision
Sudden flashes of light with new floaters
Vision loss with severe headache or eye pain

processSteps.title: Call Immediately
processSteps.description: Sudden vision loss should never wait -- call our emergency line right away.

processSteps.title: Urgent Evaluation
processSteps.description: Prompt assessment including retina and optic nerve examination.

processSteps.title: Immediate Action
processSteps.description: Treatment or referral initiated as quickly as the situation requires.

landingFaqs.question: Should I go to an emergency room instead of calling an eye clinic?
landingFaqs.answer: For most sudden vision changes, calling us first is the fastest path to the right care -- we can assess immediately and refer urgently if needed. If you're also experiencing stroke symptoms like weakness, slurred speech, or confusion, call emergency services right away.

landingFaqs.question: What if it turns out to be something minor?
landingFaqs.answer: That's a good outcome, not a wasted call. Sudden visual symptoms always deserve prompt evaluation -- we would much rather confirm something is minor than have you wait on something that wasn't.

landingFaqs.question: Can I come in today without a scheduled appointment?
landingFaqs.answer: Yes. Sudden vision changes are treated as urgent -- call us directly and we will make arrangements to see you as quickly as possible.

doctorsPerspective: Sudden vision changes are one of the few genuine emergencies in eye care, and the most important thing I can tell patients is: don't wait to see if it gets better on its own. A retinal detachment, a vascular event in the eye, or a problem affecting the optic nerve can all present this way, and the difference between calling immediately and waiting even a day can affect the outcome. If something has suddenly changed in your vision, call us -- we will tell you right away whether you need to be seen today.
